question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint (via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST with network=other) inject a crafted nodesEnvelope JSON response containing hundreds of unique serviceEndpoint entries, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to open hundreds of persistent gRPC connections to network processing nodes, increasing their connection-handling resource consumption by over 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can induce consistent transaction failures (e.g., by controlling a rogue gRPC endpoint injected via mirror node response) cause submitWithRetry() to exhaust all cfg.maxRetries+1 attempts every ticker tick, multiplying the number of CryptoTransfer transactions submitted to network processing nodes by up to 11x per interval, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls cfg.baseBackoff (via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF env var set to '1ns') cause backoff() to return near-zero sleep durations between retry attempts, effectively collapsing all maxRetries+1 attempts into a near-simultaneous burst of CryptoTransfer transactions against network processing nodes, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to the minimum valid duration (e.g., '1ns', which passes the cfg.interval <= 0 check since 1ns > 0) cause time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) to fire at near-maximum frequency, submitting CryptoTransfer transactions to network processing nodes at a rate orders of magnitude higher than the default 1s interval, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return a JSON response body of several hundred megabytes containing thousands of nodeEntry objects, causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to consume excessive CPU and memory during decoding, and then cause hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to attempt gRPC connections to thousands of network processing node addresses, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint craft a nodesEnvelope response where each nodeEntry contains the maximum number of serviceEndpoint entries all pointing to the same network processing node address, causing the pinger to register that node address many times in the network map and the SDK to preferentially route all transactions to that single node, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a rogue gRPC endpoint (injected via mirror node response) cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to always succeed but resp.GetReceipt(client) to always fail with a non-context-canceled error, causing submitWithRetry() to retry the full Execute+GetReceipt cycle cfg.maxRetries+1 times per tick, doubling the transaction submission load on network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647) cause submitWithRetry() to loop for an extremely large number of attempts per ticker tick, submitting an unbounded number of CryptoTransfer transactions to network processing nodes within a single tick interval, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user who sets cfg.baseBackoff to '1ns' and cfg.maxRetries to a large value exploit the backoff() formula (base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))) where for attempt=1 the result is 1ns, causing all retry attempts to fire with negligible delay and submit a burst of CryptoTransfer transactions to network processing nodes, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint inject serviceEndpoint entries with domain_name values that are extremely long strings (e.g., 65535 characters), causing net.JoinHostPort(host, port) to produce oversized address strings that the Hiero SDK passes to gRPC dial calls, triggering expensive DNS resolution attempts against network processing nodes and increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint inject a nodesEnvelope with hundreds of unique node_account_id values each mapped to a different network processing node address, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to establish gRPC connection pools to all of them simultaneously, increasing the aggregate resource consumption of network processing nodes by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR environment variable set it to the maximum int64 value (9223372036854775807), causing each CryptoTransfer transaction submitted by the pinger to transfer an astronomically large amount of tinybar, which may trigger more expensive validation and state-change processing on network processing nodes, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a rogue gRPC endpoint injected via mirror node response cause cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) to return a valid resp but GetReceipt(client) to block for the full SDK receipt-polling timeout before returning an error, causing each retry attempt in submitWithRetry() to hold a goroutine and gRPC connection open for an extended period, amplifying connection-level resource consumption on network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large value cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to loop for an extremely large number of HTTP attempts on startup, and if each attempt triggers a connection to a network processing node endpoint, amplify the startup-phase resource consumption of network processing nodes by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return a nodesEnvelope where the Nodes array contains thousands of nodeEntry objects each with a single serviceEndpoint pointing to the same legitimate network processing node, causing the network map to contain thousands of entries for that node and the SDK to open thousands of gRPC connections to it, increasing its resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint craft a response where grpc_proxy_endpoint fields point to legitimate network processing node addresses while service_endpoints point to attacker-controlled addresses, causing the pinger to route transactions through unexpected paths that increase processing overhead on network nodes by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ms' (1 millisecond, which is > 0 and passes validation) cause time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) to fire 1000 times per second, submitting up to 1000 CryptoTransfer transactions per second to network processing nodes without any brute-force flooding, increasing their resource consumption by over 30% compared to the default 1-per-second rate?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user who sets HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a value that toDuration() fails to parse (returning 1*time.Second as default) but then the flag.DurationVar overrides it with a very small valid duration, cause a discrepancy that results in a much higher transaction rate than intended, increasing network processing node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint inject serviceEndpoint entries with ip_address_v4 values set to broadcast or multicast addresses (e.g., 255.255.255.255), causing the Hiero SDK to attempt gRPC connections to those addresses and triggering expensive network-level processing on multiple network nodes simultaneously, increasing their aggregate resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a rogue gRPC endpoint cause Execute(client) to always return a valid TransactionID but GetReceipt(client) to return a non-terminal status (e.g., UNKNOWN) that the SDK retries internally, causing each GetReceipt call to generate multiple sub-requests to network processing nodes per retry attempt in submitWithRetry(), amplifying node resource consumption by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who sets HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '1ns' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to '63' cause the backoff() function's bit-shift expression (1<<(attempt-1)) to overflow for attempt>=64, producing a zero or negative duration that time.NewTimer treats as immediate, causing all 64 retry attempts to fire without any delay and submit 64 CryptoTransfer transactions per tick to network processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint inject a network map where all entries map to the same single network processing node account ID but with hundreds of different host:port addresses, causing the Hiero SDK to load-balance all transactions to that one node via many connections, concentrating resource consumption on that node by over 30%?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return a response with a Content-Length header indicating a small body but stream a very large body, causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to read an unbounded amount of data (since there is no body size limit on the HTTP response), consuming excessive memory and CPU that indirectly starves network processing node connections?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID environment variable set it to a smart contract account ID that triggers expensive EVM execution on network processing nodes for each CryptoTransfer, increasing their resource consumption by over 30% compared to a simple account transfer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint inject serviceEndpoint entries where domain_name is a slow-resolving DNS name (e.g., a domain with a very long TTL and slow authoritative server), causing the Hiero SDK's gRPC dial to block on DNS resolution and hold connections open to network processing nodes for extended periods, increasing their resource consumption by over 30%?
